briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 26, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensifying Global Extreme Weather and Impacts
sections.content: In 2025 and last year, various extreme weather phenomena such as wildfires, heatwaves, heavy rainfall, and droughts frequently occurred domestically, revealing their severity [1, 2]. Internationally, record-breaking March heatwaves have continued across the United States [56], impacting daily life, including the early closure of ski resorts due to high temperatures [59]. Research indicates that global greenhouse gas emissions from the United States and China between 1990 and 2020 resulted in massive economic damages totaling approximately 2경원 [52], demonstrating the severe economic cost of climate change. Climate change is projected to cause the collapse of modern agriculture and lead to more fatalities in impoverished nations [53, 66]. Amidst predictions of widespread damage, the COP30 President emphasized that climate action cannot wait for consensus [69, 79].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Climate Action and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, various educational and practical efforts to respond to climate change are actively underway. The Korea Climate Change Research Institute (KRIC) has launched the 'KRIC Climate Academy 2026' [3, 9], and the National Assembly Climate Change Forum is striving to cultivate specialized personnel by recruiting scholarship students [5]. Yeosu City is encouraging citizen participation by recruiting volunteers for the 'UNFCCC Climate Week' [4, 7, 10, 12, 16], while Uljin County [11, 13, 15, 18] and Jeonbuk Special Self-Governing Province [14] are fully launching climate change adaptation projects in the agricultural sector. Furthermore, Seongdong-gu is supporting small business owners in purchasing high-efficiency equipment [20, 24], and the Gumi National Industrial Complex is accelerating the transition to a carbon-neutral society by commencing construction on a complex facility as a leading carbon-neutral model [21, 35]. However, Hanwha Solutions, a solar and petrochemical company, faced shareholder backlash after resolving a large-scale rights offering [51], which suggests the importance of financial transparency and building market trust during the transition to eco-friendly industries.

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Expansion and Momentum for Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to a renewable energy-centric energy structure is accelerating domestically. Amidst the expansion of renewable energy, LNG is emerging as key to grid stability, emphasizing the importance of small gas turbines [40]. Muan County is exploring community-led transitions, including benchmarking resident-led renewable energy models [41, 48, 50]. Major corporations, including Samsung Electronics, have launched the Geumgang River Basin Renewable Energy Council, expanding corporate sector participation [42]. Gangneung City plans to fully implement the 'Renewable Energy Housing Support Project' in 2026 [43]. Furthermore, the movement to explicitly designate 'temperature difference energy,' such as air-source and water-source heat, as renewable energy [44], along with the exploration of floating offshore wind power initiatives [47], is broadening the technological and policy horizons of energy transition.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Change Awareness and Adaptation Strategies
sections.content: The international community is accelerating efforts to raise awareness of climate change and develop adaptation strategies. The UK Climate Minister stated the need to redefine net-zero targets [55], and France is setting a 'warming trajectory' for climate change adaptation [60]. Concrete adaptation plans are being established, such as the Valencia Port Authority developing a port adaptation plan [76]. At UC Berkeley, experiments are underway to track plant evolution in response to climate change [57], and research suggesting that 'ghost forest' phenomena could be key to understanding coastal resilience [64, 74, 75] highlights the importance of scientific approaches. However, fossil fuel companies have shown a contradictory attitude, acknowledging the climate crisis while excluding their own role [54]. Meanwhile, some politicians continue to make statements denying climate change [80, 82], hindering climate action efforts.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the urgent threat of climate change, along with the multifaceted domestic and international efforts to overcome it. Only sustained attention and participation can create a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Hanwha Solutions Approves 2.4 Trillion KRW Rights Offering, Despite No Mention at General Meeting Two Days Prior
articles.original_title: 이틀 전 주총 땐 아무 말도 안 해놓고 2조4000억원 유증 의결한 한화솔루션
articles.description: Hanwha Solutions, a solar and petrochemical company, is facing controversy after approving a large-scale rights offering worth 2.4 trillion KRW on the 26th, citing financial burdens. This decision has led to a sharp decline in its stock price due to concerns over shareholder value dilution. Shareholders who stand to be negatively impacted are strongly protesting, as the company did not disclose any plans for a rights offering at its regular general meeting held just two days prior. There are also criticisms of 'hasty processing,' as the new director approved the rights offering proposal within two days.

summary.phenomenon: Hanwha Solutions, a solar and petrochemical company, approved a large-scale rights offering of 2.4 trillion KRW on the 26th, leading to a sharp drop in its stock price due to concerns over shareholder value dilution and strong protests from shareholders. The company is facing controversy for not disclosing its rights offering plans at the regular general meeting held just two days prior.
summary.cause: Hanwha Solutions decided on the rights offering due to financial burdens, and criticisms of 'hasty processing' have arisen as a new director approved the proposal within two days.

articles.original_description: 주주가치 희석 우려에 주가 폭락태양광·석유화학 업체 한화솔루션이 재무 부담을 이유로 2조4000억원에 달하는 대규모 유상증자안을 26일 의결해 논란이 되고 있다. 불과 이틀 전 열린 정기주주총회에서 유상증자 계획을 밝히지 않아 피해를 볼 주주들이 거세게 반발하고 있다.신임 이사가 이틀 만에 유상증자안을 의결하면서 ‘졸속처리’를 했다는 지적도 제기된다. 주주···

articles.title: US and China's 30-Year Greenhouse Gas Emissions Caused $18.9 Trillion in Global Economic Damage
articles.original_title: 미·중이 30년간 뿜은 온실가스, ‘2경’짜리였다
articles.description: Researchers at Stanford University estimate global economic damage at approximately $18.9 trillion. A study has revealed that the world has suffered economic damage amounting to $18.9 trillion (approximately 28,500 trillion KRW) due to greenhouse gases emitted by the United States and China over 30 years. According to The Guardian on the 25th (local time), Stanford University researchers published in the international journal Nature that the United States, the largest carbon emitter in history, from 1990 to 2020...

summary.phenomenon: A study released on the 25th (local time) revealed that the world has incurred approximately $18.9 trillion (approximately 28,500 trillion KRW) in economic damage due to greenhouse gases emitted by the United States and China over a 30-year period, from 1990 to 2020.
summary.cause: Researchers at Stanford University stated in the international journal Nature that greenhouse gas emissions from the United States and China, historically the largest carbon emitters, are the primary cause of global economic damage.

articles.original_description: 미 스탠퍼드대 연구진, 전 세계 경제적 피해 약 19억달러로 추산미국과 중국이 30년 동안 배출한 온실가스로 전 세계가 18조9000억달러(약 2경8500조원)에 달하는 경제적 피해를 봤다는 연구 결과가 나왔다.25일(현지시간) 영국 일간 가디언에 따르면 미 스탠퍼드대 연구진은 국제학술지 네이처에 역사상 최대 탄소 배출국인 미국이 1990년부터 2020년 ···

articles.description: Exclusive: Paul Marshall also challenged over his own ‘misleading’ statements and £1.8bn of fossil fuel investments in his hedge fundThe co-owner of GB News and “committed” Christian Sir Paul Marshall has been criticised by a group of church leaders over the TV channel’s attacks on climate science and action.The hedge fund manager was also challenged over his own statements, which were called “misleading”, by the 100-strong group, which includes the former archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams
